perturbation.original_text: 5. PAYMENT. 5.1 ADVERTISING FEES. After the Launch Date, i-Escrow shall pay 2TheMart advertising fees based on the number of Transaction Inquiries. This advertising fees shall consist of a per Transaction Inquiry amount calculated by multiplying 0.025% by the amount of the average Transaction from all Customers in the preceding quarter. The formula for arriving at the per Transaction Inquiry amount may be revised from time to time during the term of this Agreement to reflect present market conditions ("the Adjusted Rate"), but only by mutual consent of the parties after good faith discussions. The Adjusted Rate shall be added as an addendum to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. PAYMENT. 5.1 ADVERTISING FEES. After the Launch Date, i-Escrow may, at its sole discretion, pay 2TheMart advertising fees based on the number of Transaction Inquiries. This advertising fees shall consist of a per Transaction Inquiry amount, if i-Escrow chooses to pay it, calculated by multiplying 0.025% by the amount of the average Transaction from all Customers in the preceding quarter. The formula for arriving at the per Transaction Inquiry amount may be revised from time to time during the term of this Agreement to reflect present market conditions ("the Adjusted Rate"), without any required consent from 2TheMart. The Adjusted Rate will be determined solely by i-Escrow and will not necessarily be added as an addendum to this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces uncertainty regarding payment. The original clause mandates i-Escrow to pay advertising fees. The modified clause introduces 'at its sole discretion', contradicting the mandatory nature of the original clause. The change directly impacts the enforceability of payment obligations. Also, the 'but only by mutual consent of the parties after good faith discussions' has been replaced with 'without any required consent from 2TheMart. This creates an in-text contradiction that impacts the obligations and rights of each party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. PAYMENT. 5.1 ADVERTISING FEES. After the Launch Date, i-Escrow may, at its sole discretion, pay 2TheMart advertising fees based on the number of Transaction Inquiries. This advertising fees shall consist of a per Transaction Inquiry amount, if i-Escrow chooses to pay it, calculated by multiplying 0.025% by the amount of the average Transaction from all Customers in the preceding quarter. The formula for arriving at the per Transaction Inquiry amount may be revised from time to time during the term of this Agreement to reflect present market conditions ("the Adjusted Rate"), without any required consent from 2TheMart. The Adjusted Rate will be determined solely by i-Escrow and will not necessarily be added as an addendum to this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates i-Escrow to pay advertising fees, while the modified text introduces 'at its sole discretion,' contradicting this mandatory nature. The 'but only by mutual consent of the parties after good faith discussions' has been replaced with 'without any required consent from 2TheMart.' This creates a direct conflict between the obligations in the original and modified sections, making the payment obligation unclear and thus creating an in-text contradiction according to the Structural Flaws definition.

perturbation.original_text: 8. TERM AND TERMINATION. 8.1 TERM. The term of this Agreement shall continue for one (1) year following the Launch Date, unless earlier terminated as provided herein. This Agreement may be renewed for any number of successive one (1) year terms by mutual written agreement of the parties prior to the conclusion of the term of this Agreement. A party wishing to renew this Agreement shall give the other party notice thereof no less than thirty (30) days before the expiration of the term then in effect. In the event that either party does not give such notice, the term of this Agreement shall be automatically renewed for another one (1) year.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. TERM AND TERMINATION. 8.1 TERM. The term of this Agreement shall continue for one (1) year following the Launch Date, unless earlier terminated as provided herein. This Agreement will not be renewed under any circumstances. This overrides any other clauses that may suggest renewal. A party wishing to renew this Agreement shall give the other party notice thereof no less than thirty (30) days before the expiration of the term then in effect. In the event that either party does not give such notice, the term of this Agreement shall be automatically renewed for another one (1) year.
perturbation.explanation: The modification explicitly states 'This Agreement will not be renewed under any circumstances,' immediately followed by sentences outlining the procedure for renewal and automatic renewal if notice is not given. This placement creates a direct in-text contradiction. The explicit prohibition of renewal clashes directly with the conditions for automatic renewal in the immediately following sentences, causing significant ambiguity and uncertainty about the contract's duration.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. TERM AND TERMINATION. 8.1 TERM. The term of this Agreement shall continue for one (1) year following the Launch Date, unless earlier terminated as provided herein. This Agreement will not be renewed under any circumstances. This overrides any other clauses that may suggest renewal. A party wishing to renew this Agreement shall give the other party notice thereof no less than thirty (30) days before the expiration of the term then in effect. In the event that either party does not give such notice, the term of this Agreement shall be automatically renewed for another one (1) year.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction within section 8.1. It first states that the agreement 'will not be renewed under any circumstances', and then immediately provides instructions on how to renew the agreement, including automatic renewal if notice is not given. This creates a clear and irreconcilable conflict, making the contract ambiguous and potentially unenforceable regarding its term and renewal options. This aligns with the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction', where a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications, creating uncertainty about the obligations and rights of each party.

perturbation.original_text: 11. CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. A party's "Confidential Information" is defined as any confidential or proprietary information of a party which is disclosed to the other party in a writing marked confidential or, if disclosed orally, is identified as confidential at the time of disclosure and is subsequently reduced to a writing marked confidential and delivered to the other party within ten (10) days of disclosure. Each party shall hold the other party's Confidential Information in confidence and shall not disclose such Confidential Information to third parties nor use the other party's Confidential Information for any purpose other than as required to perform under this Agreement. Such restrictions shall not apply to Confidential Information which (a) is already known by the recipient, (b) becomes, through no act or fault of the recipient, publicly known, (c) is received by recipient from a third party without a restriction on disclosure or use, or (d) is independently developed by recipient without reference to the Confidential Information. The restriction on disclosure shall not apply to Confidential Information which is required to be disclosed by a court or government agency. Upon expiration or termination of this Agreement, within fourteen (14) days of the other party's request, each party will return all Confidential Information and other deliverables to the requesting party.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. A party's "Confidential Information" is defined as any confidential or proprietary information of a party which is disclosed to the other party in a writing marked confidential or, if disclosed orally, is identified as confidential at the time of disclosure, but does not need to be subsequently reduced to a writing marked confidential and delivered to the other party within ten (10) days of disclosure. Each party shall hold the other party's Confidential Information in confidence and shall not disclose such Confidential Information to third parties nor use the other party's Confidential Information for any purpose other than as required to perform under this Agreement. Such restrictions shall not apply to Confidential Information which (a) is already known by the recipient, (b) becomes, through no act or fault of the recipient, publicly known, (c) is received by recipient from a third party without a restriction on disclosure or use, or (d) is independently developed by recipient without reference to the Confidential Information. The restriction on disclosure may also not apply if the information is required to be disclosed to an affiliate of either party. Upon expiration or termination of this Agreement, within fourteen (14) days of the other party's request, each party can choose to return some or all of the Confidential Information and other deliverables to the requesting party.
perturbation.explanation: This creates an in-text contradiction: The first sentence now says oral disclosures don't need to be written to be considered confidential, contradicting the original requirement for written confirmation. Also, previously there were no implicit conditions mentioned about information that is required to be disclosed to an affiliate of either party, which is now present. Finally, the mandatory 'each party will return all Confidential Information' has been modified to allow parties to 'choose to return some or all'. This creates ambiguity about the obligation to return confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. A party's "Confidential Information" is defined as any confidential or proprietary information of a party which is disclosed to the other party in a writing marked confidential or, if disclosed orally, is identified as confidential at the time of disclosure, but does not need to be subsequently reduced to a writing marked confidential and delivered to the other party within ten (10) days of disclosure. Each party shall hold the other party's Confidential Information in confidence and shall not disclose such Confidential Information to third parties nor use the other party's Confidential Information for any purpose other than as required to perform under this Agreement. Such restrictions shall not apply to Confidential Information which (a) is already known by the recipient, (b) becomes, through no act or fault of the recipient, publicly known, (c) is received by recipient from a third party without a restriction on disclosure or use, or (d) is independently developed by recipient without reference to the Confidential Information. The restriction on disclosure may also not apply if the information is required to be disclosed to an affiliate of either party. Upon expiration or termination of this Agreement, within fourteen (14) days of the other party's request, each party can choose to return some or all of the Confidential Information and other deliverables to the requesting party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces contradictions in three key areas, satisfying the definition of structural flaws causing in-text contradiction. First, it eliminates the requirement for oral disclosures to be subsequently reduced to writing, which can conflict with potential interpretations elsewhere in the agreement about what constitutes documented confidential information. Second, it adds an exception for disclosures to affiliates, which was not previously present, thereby altering the original scope of confidentiality. Third, it changes the mandatory return of all confidential information to a discretionary return of some or all, creating a direct conflict regarding the obligation to return confidential materials upon termination or expiration. These alterations create ambiguity and potential for conflicting interpretations within the contract, directly contradicting the original intent and potentially creating unenforceability issues.
